description: Improve document formatting for neat, professional output matching PLN official contract format. Fix the _direct_variable_fill() method and LLM prompts to produce clean HTML with proper spacing between paragraphs, clear separation between Pasal sections, correct numbering (1, 2, 3 for main items; a, b, c for sub-items), proper indentation, and visual separators between sections. The output should look like a professional legal document.
steps:
1. Rewrite backend/app/services/drafting_service.py _direct_variable_fill() method to produce properly structured HTML: (a) Add CSS classes for consistent styling: 'contract-document', 'pasal-section', 'pasal-title', 'pasal-content'. (b) Add margin-top: 24px before each h2 (Pasal heading) for clear separation between sections. (c) Add a horizontal rule
between Pasal sections. (d) Properly detect and format nested numbered lists: main numbering (1. 2. 3.) as
    , sub-numbering (a. b. c.) as
      . (e) Wrap each paragraph in

      . (f) Add proper spacing and indentation to list items. (g) Handle multi-line clause text properly (split on double newlines for paragraphs, single newlines within numbered items).

2. Update backend/app/rag/prompts.py: Update HTML_FORMAT_INSTRUCTIONS and the SINGLE_CLAUSE_ASSEMBLY_PROMPT / FULL_DOCUMENT_ASSEMBLY_PROMPT to instruct the LLM to produce well-formatted HTML with: proper spacing, separators between Pasals, nested lists, justified text, and margin-bottom on paragraphs. Add explicit formatting rules about list indentation and paragraph spacing.

3. Update the LLM system prompt (LEGAL_DRAFTING_SYSTEM_PROMPT) in backend/app/rag/prompts.py to include explicit instructions about document neatness: margin-bottom on paragraphs (12px), line-height 1.6, text-align justify, <hr> separator between sections, proper nested <ol> for sub-items.

4. Add inline styles to the generated HTML in _direct_variable_fill() for the contract title <h1> (center-aligned, font-size 18px, margin-bottom 24px), section headings <h2> (font-size 14px, font-weight bold, margin-top 24px, margin-bottom 12px), and ensure list items have consistent line-height and spacing.

5. Update frontend/src/lib/components/TipTapEditor.svelte: Add a <style> block with CSS rules for the .tiptap-editor content: style h1, h2, p, ol, li, hr elements to match the contract formatting (proper spacing, indentation, line-height). Ensure nested lists display with proper indentation and numbering styles (decimal for top-level, lower-alpha for nested).

6. Update backend/tests/test_drafting.py: Add test for _direct_variable_fill format output - verify it produces HTML with pasal-separator hr tags, proper ol nesting, margin styles on paragraphs, and section structure.
acceptance_criteria: Generated HTML has clear visual separation (hr element or large margin) between each Pasal section
Paragraphs within a section have consistent 12px margin-bottom and 1.6 line-height
Numbered lists use proper <ol> with type attributes: type='1' for main items, type='a' for sub-items
The contract title is centered with larger font
TipTap editor renders the formatted HTML with proper spacing and indentation
The _direct_variable_fill fallback also produces neat HTML (not just the LLM path)
Tests verify the formatting structure of generated HTML
verification:
cd /projects/sandbox/PengembanganAI-CLMS/backend && python -c "from app.services.drafting_service import DraftingService; ds = DraftingService.__new__(DraftingService); clauses = [{'metadata': {'pasal_number': 'Pasal 1', 'section_name': 'DEFINISI'}, 'document': 'Isi: Item 1.
2. Item 2
a. Sub A
b. Sub B'}]; result = ds._direct_variable_fill(clauses, {}); assert 'pasal-separator' in result or 'margin-top' in result; assert '<ol' in result; print('Format OK')"
